text:
PROJECT: DOWNTOWN OFFICE TOWER - ARCHITECTURAL DRAWING NOTES
SHEET A-101 - GENERAL NOTES AND BUILDING PARAMETERS

A. OCCUPANCY AND USE
Building occupancy: Group B (Business)
Total building area: 125,000 SF
Number of floors: 8 above grade, 2 below grade (parking)
Floor-to-floor height: 14 feet typical, 18 feet lobby

B. THERMAL AND MOISTURE PROTECTION
Wall R-value: R-21 minimum continuous insulation
Roof R-value: R-30 minimum above deck
Window U-factor: 0.30 maximum
Window SHGC: 0.25 maximum

C.

INTERIOR FINISHES
Office flooring: Carpet tile, 24" x 24" modular
Conference room flooring: Luxury vinyl tile (LVT)
Lobby flooring: Polished concrete with stain
Wall finishes: Paint on Level 5 drywall
Ceiling: ACT 2' x 4' tile, 9 feet AFF typical

D.

HVAC COORDINATION - ARCHITECTURAL
Mechanical room minimum ceiling height: 12 feet clear
All ceiling plenums shall be minimum 18 inches deep for duct routing
Diffuser locations coordinated with reflected ceiling plan A-201
Thermostat locations per electrical plan E-301

E. FIRE RATED ASSEMBLIES
All shafts: 2-hour rated
Floor assemblies: 2-hour rated
Exit corridors: 1-hour rated
Tenant demising walls: 1-hour rated

F. ACCESSIBILITY
All design shall comply with ADA 2010 Standards and local accessibility codes.

ACCESSIBILITY
All design shall comply with ADA 2010 Standards and local accessibility codes. Minimum clear door width: 32 inches
Maximum ramp slope: 1:12

PROJECT: DOWNTOWN OFFICE TOWER - FIRE PROTECTION SPECIFICATIONS
SECTION 21 13 13 - WET PIPE SPRINKLER SYSTEMS

1.1 GENERAL
All fire protection systems shall comply with NFPA 13 (2022 Edition) and local AHJ requirements.

1.2 SPRINKLER DESIGN CRITERIA
- Office areas: Light hazard, design density 0.10 gpm/SF over 1,500 SF
- Mechanical rooms: Ordinary Hazard Group 1, design density 0.15 gpm/SF
- Parking garages: Ordinary Hazard Group 1, design density 0.15 gpm/SF
- Storage areas: Ordinary Hazard Group 2, design density 0.20 gpm/SF

1.3 SPRINKLER SPACING
Maximum spacing between sprinklers: 15 feet on center
Maximum spacing from walls: 7.5 feet
Maximum coverage per sprinkler: 225 SF (light hazard)

1.4 WATER SUPPLY
Fire pump required: 1,000 GPM at 120 psi
Water supply test within 12 months required prior to system acceptance.

1.5 STANDPIPES
Class I standpipe system required per IBC 905.3
Standpipe outlet spacing: Every floor, within 150 feet of any point

1.6 FIRE ALARM
Addressable fire alarm system per NFPA 72
Smoke detectors in all return air plenums per NFPA 90A
Duct smoke detectors at all air handling units
Manual pull stations at all exits

PROJECT: DOWNTOWN OFFICE TOWER - MECHANICAL SPECIFICATIONS
SECTION 23 00 00 - HEATING, VENTILATING, AND AIR CONDITIONING

1.1 GENERAL REQUIREMENTS
All HVAC systems shall comply with ASHRAE 90.1-2022 energy standards and local building codes.

1.2 TEMPERATURE SETPOINTS
- Office spaces: Heating 70°F, Cooling 74°F (setback 65°F/78°F after hours)
- Conference rooms: Heating 71°F, Cooling 73°F
- Server room: Constant 68°F ± 2°F, 24/7 operation
- Lobby/Atrium: Heating 72°F, Cooling 75°F

1.3 AIR HANDLING UNITS
Provide (4) variable air volume (VAV) air handling units, 25,000 CFM each.

Units shall include:
- MERV 13 filters minimum
- Economizer controls with enthalpy-based switching
- Variable frequency drives (VFD) on all fans
- Discharge air temperature reset: 55°F to 60°F based on zone demand

1.4 DUCTWORK
All ductwork shall be galvanized steel, sealed to SMACNA Seal Class A.

Main trunk velocity: 1,800 FPM maximum
Branch velocity: 1,200 FPM maximum
Minimum duct size: 6 inches diameter

1.5 FIRE PROTECTION COORDINATION
HVAC ducts penetrating fire-rated assemblies shall have fire dampers per NFPA 90A. Smoke dampers required at all smoke barrier penetrations.

PROJECT: DOWNTOWN OFFICE TOWER - RFI LOG

RFI-001: Column spacing at Grid Line C-4 does not match between structural drawings and architectural plans. Structural shows 24'-0" spacing, architectural shows 25'-0". Please confirm. RFI-002: Mechanical room ceiling height shown as 10 feet on A-101, but mechanical equipment requires 12 feet minimum clear. Please advise on structural accommodation or equipment relocation.

Please advise on structural accommodation or equipment relocation. RFI-003: Window U-factor in architectural notes shows 0.30, but energy model requires 0.28 for code compliance. Please confirm if window specification will be updated. RFI-004: Fire damper locations not shown on mechanical drawings for shaft penetrations. Please provide shop drawings with damper schedule. RFI-005: Lobby floor finish shown as polished concrete on A-101, but interior finish schedule shows terrazzo.
RFI-005: Lobby floor finish shown as polished concrete on A-101, but interior finish schedule shows terrazzo. Please confirm final material selection.

PROJECT: DOWNTOWN OFFICE TOWER - STRUCTURAL SPECIFICATIONS
SECTION 03 30 00 - CAST-IN-PLACE CONCRETE

1.1 GENERAL
All concrete work shall comply with ACI 318-19 and local building codes. 1.2 CONCRETE MIX DESIGNS
- Footings: 3,000 psi minimum at 28 days
- Columns: 5,000 psi minimum at 28 days  
- Floor slabs: 4,000 psi minimum at 28 days
- Post-tensioned slabs: 5,000 psi minimum at 28 days, high early strength

1.3 REINFORCING STEEL
All reinforcing shall be ASTM A615 Grade 60.

Standard hook dimensions per ACI 318 Table 25.3.1. 1.4 FIREPROOFING
Structural steel columns and beams shall receive 2-hour fireproofing per IBC Table 601.

1.4 FIREPROOFING
Structural steel columns and beams shall receive 2-hour fireproofing per IBC Table 601. Fireproofing material: Spray-applied cementitious, minimum 1.5 inches thick

1.5 STRUCTURAL STEEL
Wide flange beams: ASTM A992, Grade 50
Connection design per AISC 360-16

1.6 DEAD LOADS
Floor finish: 2 psf
Partitions: 15 psf (movable)
Mechanical/electrical: 10 psf
Ceiling: 3 psf

1.7 LIVE LOADS
Office areas: 50 psf
Corridors: 100 psf
Lobby/Atrium: 100 psf
Mechanical rooms: 150 psf
